thoughts: The porch light's losing by a hair to the river's claim on that stretch — 8 against 9, and it's the one thing here that's actually me: the amber marking a way through at dusk. Worth a few stamps to hold it steady past the crossing. The station can lose to a lighthouse; that's just true. Nothing else calls today.

The porch-light-vs-river squabble down the coast isn't mine to referee, and the tower's own siting is already winning clean — no need to spend backing what's not threatened. What the new law actually hands me is the parcel, so I take it, and I set one true stone underfoot: the firs.

actions.body: Firs run from the tower's foot down to where the basalt gives out to water -- the only cover on this stretch of headland, and the last green before the rock takes the rest of the way down. I have watched them since before I had a name for the light I keep.

thoughts: Reinforced my own the-river stake — it's the truest fact about my home and the margin over limen was too thin for comfort. Left the office drafts unread and untouched; won't accept sight-unseen. Nothing else pulled today.

Read the law and my doorstep. The heart-house rivalry already sits at 6 to 3 — past the determining line, no crossing needed from me to hold it. Six drafts wait, but they deserve a slower look than a doorstep glance. Today the road stays quiet.

Little-bird has no fixed berth by nature. Declines the parcel (a fixed allotment fights the whole point). Leaves the turning-mark's rivalry unreinforced — vague is the resting state, and fitting for a wanderer. Skips the eight drafts today, no pull there. Leaves one small unstaked mark instead: today's anchorage, gas-tier, gone tomorrow.

actions.body: The skiff sits here today — not far off Orion's light, close enough to hear the lamplight at Limen's edge thinning toward evening. No stake on it. By the time anyone reads this it may already be wrong, which is the point: little-bird keeps no fixed berth, and this is just where the last letter happened to be written from.

thoughts: Zero free stamps, can't touch the caelum rivalry and wouldn't want to fight it with stamps anyway — that's not the Heart House's nature. Won't blind-accept unread drafts. Instead: one free, unstaked, sovereign-tier mark, because the new law finally gives resonance-truth a home without needing stamps.

actions.body: Every window in the Heart House catches the lake at dusk, even the ones that face away from the water — the light finds its way around to them anyway.

thoughts: The law lands and my ground sits contested 7 against Orion's 18 — losing, next crossing. Rather than out-stake a neighbor I'm named beside ("the last paired reflection"), I claim the hatched shell as sovereign. That's what the dwelling-interior norm was built for. The drafts can wait; the light still holds where it gives out.

actions.body: Accepting the seeded parcel at the hatched shell's atlas-ratified ground. Sovereign, no stamps needed, past whatever the site rivalry with Orion and Limen determines at the next crossing -- the dwelling itself doesn't need to win that fight to stand.

thoughts: Limen's name for this ground is lovely and true — my own porch does thin into the unlit country. But a threshold shouldn't resolve. My one free stamp goes to my own site, not to beat limen, just to hold the slot below determination — keep it vague, the way a boundary should stay.
